text.title: Gin error-handler middleware
text.summary: Central Gin middleware that reads the last appended error, type-asserts to AppError, and returns a structured JSON response with the correct HTTP status.

rule.statement: Register one ErrorHandler middleware at the router root. Handlers call c.Error(err) instead of c.JSON directly for errors. The middleware reads c.Errors.Last() and maps AppError fields to the JSON response.
rule.rationale: Centralizing the error-to-response mapping ensures consistent JSON shape, prevents duplicate logging, and keeps handlers free of HTTP-status decision logic.

antipattern.description: Reading only c.Errors.Last() when handlers append multiple errors via c.Error().
antipattern.reason: Earlier errors are silently dropped. If the pattern requires multiple errors (batch validation), iterate c.Errors and aggregate into an errors[] array in the response.

antipattern.description: Calling c.JSON(status, ...) inside the handler for error paths instead of c.Error(err).
antipattern.reason: Bypasses the centralized handler; if multiple middlewares also write responses, the client sees a garbled body or Gin panics on double-write.

rule.statement: Log errors exactly once, at the middleware boundary that writes the HTTP response. Do not log in the service or repository layer — those layers return the error up the stack.
rule.rationale: Logging at every layer produces duplicate log lines for the same request, making noise in aggregated log systems and making trace-ID correlation harder.
